metadata.title: Versatility Braves Dust-Storm Seeking Evidence of Water
metadata.curiosityNote: Solar-powered rovers ration their work when dust storms block sunlight from the panels.

story.briefTemplate: Solar-powered Mars rover Versatility was sent to investigate a clay ridge where ancient water may have once flowed. In a productive morning, Versatility captured {quantity:morning_photos} of terrain on the ridge, but by afternoon a rust-colored dust storm blew over the ridge, completely blocking sunlight from reaching the rover's solar panels. Bunkering down to save power, the rover was still {quantity:photos_shy_after_storm} shy of its photo target for the day. When the dust settled and sunlight returned, the rover was able to take {quantity:closeup_photos_after_storm} of a strange glittering rock.
story.causalEvent: A dust storm blocked sunlight from the rover's solar panels.
story.closingNoteTemplate: The rover log closed with Versatility still {quantity:final_photo_shortfall} short of its daily target.

label.child: Photos short of the target after the storm
label.lowercase: the photos shy after the storm

label.child: Close-up photos after the dust cleared
label.lowercase: the close-up photos after the storm

label.child: Photos still short of the target

steps.prompt: What was Versatility's target number of photos for the day?
steps.reasoningPrompt: The rover took some photos and was still short of the target. How do the photos taken and the missing photos relate to the target?

backwardCheck.prompt: Take the morning photos back out. Do you land on the photos it was still short?

steps.prompt: Including the round of photos after the storm, how many photos short of its daily goal was Versatility?
steps.reasoningPrompt: The rover was short of its goal, then took more photos after the storm. Did the shortfall get bigger or smaller?

backwardCheck.prompt: Put the close-up photos back on. Do you return to the first shortfall?

operatorExperiments.alternateWorldTemplate: This matches the story: {label:morning_photos.lowercase} and {label:photos_shy_after_storm.lowercase} combine into {label:daily_photo_target.lowercase}.
Subtracting would fit a different story where {quantity:photos_shy_after_storm} were taken away instead of counted toward the target.
This would fit a different question: if there were {value:photos_shy_after_storm} groups like {label:morning_photos.lowercase}, how many photos would there be?
This would fit a different question about sharing {label:morning_photos.lowercase} into equal groups.
This matches the story: the close-up photos after the storm came off the shortfall, closing the gap.
Adding would fit a different story where {quantity:closeup_photos_after_storm} widened the shortfall instead of closing it.
This would fit a different question: if there were {value:closeup_photos_after_storm} groups like {label:photos_shy_after_storm.lowercase}, how many photos would there be?
This would fit a different question about sharing {label:photos_shy_after_storm.lowercase} into equal groups.

recap.headline: Why Versatility ended {quantity:final_photo_shortfall} short
causalChain: The storm left it {quantity:photos_shy_after_storm} short of its target.
After sunlight returned, {quantity:closeup_photos_after_storm} reduced the shortfall to {quantity:final_photo_shortfall}.

dataQuestion.prompt: What does {quantity:photos_shy_after_storm} represent in the rover model?
dataQuestion.correctFeedback: Right. {quantity:photos_shy_after_storm} is how far Versatility was from its daily photo target after the storm.
dataQuestion.incorrectFeedback: That amount is the rover's shortfall after the storm: {quantity:photos_shy_after_storm}.
